CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. 2026-04-08T08:57:00.6847540Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-08T08:55:05.547937981Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57926,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Document new ResponseEventStream convenience generators in README and CHANGELOG\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57895 by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57895 added new **convenience generators** on `ResponseEventStream` — a middle ground between `TextResponse` (simple text-only) and the full builder API (`AddOutputItemMessage()` etc.):\\n\\n- `stream.OutputItemMessage(string text)` — emits a complete text message (all inner events) in a single call\\n- `stream.OutputItemMessage(IAsyncEnumerable tokens, CancellationToken)` — streams token-by-token with each chunk becoming a `response.output_text.delta` event\\n- `stream.OutputItemFunctionCall(name, callId, arguments)` — emits a complete function call output item in one call\\n- `stream.OutputItemReasoningItem(...)` — emits a reasoning output item\\n\\nThe samples (Sample 3, 4, 6) were restructured to lead with convenience-first patterns, but the **main README** and **CHANGELOG** were not updated to reflect these new APIs.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The main `README.md` \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section documents only `TextResponse` and the full builder control pattern (`AddOutputItemMessage()` / `AddTextContent()` / `EmitAdded()` etc.) — the convenience generators (`stream.OutputItemMessage()`, `stream.OutputItemFunctionCall()`, `stream.OutputItemReasoningItem()`) are invisible to a developer reading only the README\\n- The CHANGELOG (`CHANGELOG.md`) has only the initial release entry and does not mention any of the convenience generators added in PR #57895\\n- The README description of `ResponseEventStream` should clarify when to use convenience generators vs. the full builder API (currently missing entirely from the key concepts section)\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — EDIT\\n\\nIn the **Key concepts → ResponseHandler** section, after the existing `ResponseEventStream — full control over every SSE event:` subsection, add a new subsection documenting convenience generators. Replace the current `ResponseEventStream` description block (approximately lines 57–81) with an expanded version:\\n\\n```markdown\\n**`ResponseEventStream` — convenience generators (recommended for most cases):**\\n\\nWhen `TextResponse` is too simple but the full builder API is more than you need, use the convenience generators on `ResponseEventStream`. They handle all inner events (`output_item.added`, `content_part.added`, deltas, `content_part.done`, `output_item.done`) automatically:\\n\\n```\\npublic class GreetingHandler : ResponseHandler\\n{\\n    public override async IAsyncEnumerable CreateAsync(\\n        CreateResponse request,\\n        ResponseContext context,\\n        [EnumeratorCancellation] CancellationToken cancellationToken)\\n    {\\n        await Task.CompletedTask;\\n        var stream = new ResponseEventStream(context, request);\\n\\n        yield return stream.EmitCreated();\\n        yield return stream.EmitInProgress();\\n\\n        // One call emits all text output events automatically.\\n        var input = request.GetInputText();\\n        foreach (var evt in stream.OutputItemMessage($\\u0026#34;Hello! You said: \\\\\\u0026#34;{input}\\\\\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;))\\n            yield return evt;\\n\\n        yield return stream.EmitCompleted();\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nFor token-by-token streaming, pass an `IAsyncEnumerable`:\\n\\n```\\nawait foreach (var evt in stream.OutputItemMessage(tokenSourceAsync, cancellationToken))\\n    yield return evt;\\n```\\n\\nFor function calling, use `OutputItemFunctionCall()`:\\n\\n```\\nforeach (var evt in stream.OutputItemFunctionCall(\\u0026#34;get_weather\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;call_1\\u0026#34;, arguments))\\n    yield return evt;\\n```\\n\\n**`ResponseEventStream` — full builder control:**\\n\\nUse the builder API only when you need to interleave non-event work *between individual delta/done calls within a content part*, or set custom properties on an output item before `EmitAdded()`:\\n\\n```\\n// ... (existing code) ...\\n```\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/CHANGELOG.md` — EDIT\\n\\nAdd a new entry under `## 1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` in `### Features Added`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- Added `ResponseEventStream` convenience generators for emitting complete output items in one call:\\n  - `OutputItemMessage(string text)` — emits a full text message output item (handles all inner SSE events automatically)\\n  - `OutputItemMessage(IAsyncEnumerable tokens, CancellationToken)` — streams tokens as `response.output_text.delta` events\\n  - `OutputItemFunctionCall(name, callId, arguments)` — emits a complete function call output item\\n  - `OutputItemReasoningItem(...)` — emits a reasoning output item\\n- Samples restructured for progressive complexity: convenience-first patterns in Samples 3, 4, and 6 with full builder variants for advanced use cases.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe convenience generator snippets referenced in Step 1 need corresponding `#region` / `#endregion` blocks in a sample test file.\\n\\n1. **Write the sample code** in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Samples/Sample0_ReadMe.cs` (create if it doesn\\u0026#39;t exist, or add to an existing ReadMe sample file)\\n\\n   For each snippet (`Responses_ReadMe_ConvenienceHandler`, `Responses_ReadMe_StreamingConvenienceHandler`, `Responses_ReadMe_FunctionCallConvenience`), wrap the handler class or usage snippet with:\\n   ```csharp\\n   #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ConvenienceHandler\\n   // ... handler code ...\\n   #endregion\\n   ```\\n\\n2. **Add snippet placeholders in README.md** at the exact locations described in Step 1:\\n   ````markdown\\n   ```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ConvenienceHandler\\n   ```\\n   ````\\n\\n3. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` to inject the actual code.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already has all required sections:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Configure the server)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts — **INCOMPLETE**: missing convenience generator documentation\\n3. ✅ Examples (links to samples)\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\nOnly the **Key concepts** section needs updating as described in Step 1.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — injects snippet code into README placeholders; check `git diff` to confirm README updated\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — regenerates public API listing if any public types changed\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; section documents only `TextResponse` and the full builder control pattern (`AddOutputItemMessage()` / `AddTextContent()` / `EmitAdded()` etc.) — the convenience generators (`stream.OutputItemMessage()`, `stream.OutputItemFunctionCall()`, `stream.OutputItemReasoningItem()`) are invisible to a developer reading only the README\\n- The CHANGELOG (`CHANGELOG.md`) has only the initial release entry and does not mention any of the convenience generators added in PR #57895\\n- The README description of `ResponseEventStream` should clarify when to use convenience generators vs. the full builder API (currently missing entirely from the key concepts section)\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — EDIT\\n\\nIn the **Key concepts → ResponseHandler** section, after the existing `ResponseEventStream — full control over every SSE event:` subsection, add a new subsection documenting convenience generators. Replace the current `ResponseEventStream` description block (approximately lines 57–81) with an expanded version:\\n\\n```markdown\\n**`ResponseEventStream` — convenience generators (recommended for most cases):**\\n\\nWhen `TextResponse` is too simple but the full builder API is more than you need, use the convenience generators on `ResponseEventStream`. They handle all inner events (`output_item.added`, `content_part.added`, deltas, `content_part.done`, `output_item.done`) automatically:\\n\\n```\\npublic class GreetingHandler : ResponseHandler\\n{\\n    public override async IAsyncEnumerable CreateAsync(\\n        CreateResponse request,\\n        ResponseContext context,\\n        [EnumeratorCancellation] CancellationToken cancellationToken)\\n    {\\n        await Task.CompletedTask;\\n        var stream = new ResponseEventStream(context, request);\\n\\n        yield return stream.EmitCreated();\\n        yield return stream.EmitInProgress();\\n\\n        // One call emits all text output events automatically.\\n        var input = request.GetInputText();\\n        foreach (var evt in stream.OutputItemMessage($\\u0026#34;Hello! You said: \\\\\\u0026#34;{input}\\\\\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;))\\n            yield return evt;\\n\\n        yield return stream.EmitCompleted();\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nFor token-by-token streaming, pass an `IAsyncEnumerable`:\\n\\n```\\nawait foreach (var evt in stream.OutputItemMessage(tokenSourceAsync, cancellationToken))\\n    yield return evt;\\n```\\n\\nFor function calling, use `OutputItemFunctionCall()`:\\n\\n```\\nforeach (var evt in stream.OutputItemFunctionCall(\\u0026#34;get_weather\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;call_1\\u0026#34;, arguments))\\n    yield return evt;\\n```\\n\\n**`ResponseEventStream` — full builder control:**\\n\\nUse the builder API only when you need to interleave non-event work *between individual delta/done calls within a content part*, or set custom properties on an output item before `EmitAdded()`:\\n\\n```\\n// ... (existing code) ...\\n```\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/CHANGELOG.md` — EDIT\\n\\nAdd a new entry under `## 1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` in `### Features Added`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- Added `ResponseEventStream` convenience generators for emitting complete output items in one call:\\n  - `OutputItemMessage(string text)` — emits a full text message output item (handles all inner SSE events automatically)\\n  - `OutputItemMessage(IAsyncEnumerable tokens, CancellationToken)` — streams tokens as `response.output_text.delta` events\\n  - `OutputItemFunctionCall(name, callId, arguments)` — emits a complete function call output item\\n  - `OutputItemReasoningItem(...)` — emits a reasoning output item\\n- Samples restructured for progressive complexity: convenience-first patterns in Samples 3, 4, and 6 with full builder variants for advanced use cases.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe convenience generator snippets referenced in Step 1 need corresponding `#region` / `#endregion` blocks in a sample test file.\\n\\n1. **Write the sample code** in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Samples/Sample0_ReadMe.cs` (create if it doesn\\u0026#39;t exist, or add to an existing ReadMe sample file)\\n\\n   For each snippet (`Responses_ReadMe_ConvenienceHandler`, `Responses_ReadMe_StreamingConvenienceHandler`, `Responses_ReadMe_FunctionCallConvenience`), wrap the handler class or usage snippet with:\\n   ```csharp\\n   #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ConvenienceHandler\\n   // ... handler code ...\\n   #endregion\\n   ```\\n\\n2. **Add snippet placeholders in README.md** at the exact locations described in Step 1:\\n   ````markdown\\n   ```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ConvenienceHandler\\n   ```\\n   ````\\n\\n3. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` to inject the actual code.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already has all required sections:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Configure the server)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts — **INCOMPLETE**: missing convenience generator documentation\\n3. ✅ Examples (links to samples)\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\nOnly the **Key concepts** section needs updating as described in Step 1.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — injects snippet code into README placeholders; check `git diff` to confirm README updated\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — regenerates public API listing if any public types changed\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt;
